steps.title: Identify the word order in each choice
steps.content: The forms ending in it was invented have standard word order: subject before verb. The forms wasn't it and was it have inverted word order: auxiliary verb before subject.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
The rule: Inverted word order (the syntax of questions) requires a question mark, not a period.

The correct answer correctly pairs standard word order with a period — the proper form for a statement.

The other choices either incorrectly pair inverted word order with a period (C and D, violating the requirement that question syntax must use a question mark) or pair standard word order with a question mark (B, creating an unwarranted interrogative tone).

That confirms A is correct.
